sourceText: Time flows and much changes, representing the transience of human affairs in contrast to the unchanging nature.
All that is is from the One.
This is the core idea of Neoplatonism, stating that all individual beings and phenomena emanate from the ultimate source, the 'One'.
The soul is not trapped in the body, but is a path to the divine through the body.
This implies that the body should not be viewed negatively, but rather as part of the soul's journey towards the 'One'.
All beauty we perceive is a shadow of the 'Good'.
This means that the beauty of all things in the world is a faint reflection of the 'One', the ultimate source of goodness, and through it, we can perceive the 'One'.
I want to write operas that will make people weep.
Puccini aimed to create powerful and moving dramas that evoked deep human emotions, particularly sorrow and empathy, rather than mere entertainment.
Music is the language of emotion, and sometimes it speaks more than words can.
Puccini believed that complex human emotions could be subtly conveyed through melody and harmony, a belief his operas vividly demonstrate.
The most beautiful melodies are often born from the deepest sorrow.
He suggests that personal pain and hardship can be a source of artistic inspiration, implying that the poignant beauty found in his works stems from such experiences.

David IV ascended the throne at just 16 to a kingdom devastated by Seljuk Turk invasions and torn apart by internal strife among powerful, rebellious nobles. His nation was forced to pay a humiliating tribute, and its very existence was threatened by constant raids and political fragmentation.
